title: calophyllaceae
intro: The Calophyllaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, also known as the Calophyllaceae family or the family Calophyllaceae. This family consists of approximately 280 species of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs, primarily found in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. The Calophyllaceae family is characterized by its unique combination of characteristics, including alternate, simple leaves and small, apetalous flowers. Members of this family are often used in traditional medicine and have potential for sustainable timber production.
